title: container restart fails when using pasta + port publish
body:
### Issue Description

`podman restart <container>` fails with:
```
WARN[0010] StopSignal SIGTERM failed to stop container test in 10 seconds, resorting to SIGKILL 
Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Failed to bind port 2222 (Address already in use) for option '-t 2222-2222:22-22', exiting
```
when using `pasta` with published port. Works fine with `slirp4netns` though

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create container:
```
podman create --name test --network pasta --publish 2222:22 docker.io/fedora sleep 60
```
2. Start container:
```
podman start test
```
3. Restart container:
```
podman restart test
```

### Describe the results you received

Restart fails:
```
Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Failed to bind port 2222 (Address already in use) for option '-t 2222-2222:22-22', exiting
```

### Describe the results you expected

Restart succeeds.

comments.body:
Did you run out of inotify instances for you user by chance? Can you check if you see ...`use a timer` in your journal written by pasta?

Fundamentally the netns quit code is racy, not just for pasta but slirp4netns and rootlessport as well...
For pasta it exits when we unmount the netns path (either via inotify or 1s poll interval), so when ports are bound and we restart we may launch pasta before the old pasta exited. The use of the timer make it of course much easier to reproduce...

With slirp4netns and rootlessport it is better because we send a pipe down into conmon and once conmon closes the pipe they will exit. The close of the pipe happens directly after the container died so chances of a race are much more unlikely as the window until the restart is much longer compared to the step where we unmount the netns. However it still is racy.

Overall I think the right fix would be to track the pid of the processes and SIGKILL them in container teardown. But SIGKILL is not synchronous either so we still would wait for the exit somehow. We have problems of potential pid reuse, when the network proces exits podman cannot notice this as we are not a deamon, then another process might get the same pid and we start killing the wrong process. We do have such problems elsewhere so it is already an accepted risk I would say.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I can reproduce the same issue here, and the journal seems not showing `use a timer`.

Currently I avoid restarting a container using podman's mechanism. I instead just timeout a `.container` in quadlet, and systemd's restart will work like one stop and one start.

This error consistently happens on all my containers that expose a port via pasta. The workaround has been spinning up pods for each container, grouping them up when it makes sense. There is very little reason to restart pods except when I am changing things, and things have been stable so far.

I get the same behavior when doing a "restart". However, if I execute instead ```podman stop test && podman start test```, the container behaves as expected.

Edit: which, I see, [is already reported](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27634#issuecomment-3617464502).

In my case, I am not using selinux but apparmor. Should I tear apparmor completely down (although the module is loaded in the kernel), the problem still shows up. And there is no record in /var/log/audit.log (as answering [this comment](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27901#issuecomment-3755080499))

However: if I add apparmor=0 to the cmdline, then it is disabled... but I still get the issue:

```
ubuntu@k3s:~$ sudo aa-enabled
No - disabled at boot.
ubuntu@k3s:~$ podman start test
test
ubuntu@k3s:~$ podman restart test
WARN[0010] StopSignal SIGTERM failed to stop container test in 10 seconds, resorting to SIGKILL 
test
ubuntu@k3s:~$ podman start test
test
ubuntu@k3s:~$ podman restart test
WARN[0010] StopSignal SIGTERM failed to stop container test in 10 seconds, resorting to SIGKILL 
Error: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Failed to bind port 2222 (Address already in use) for option '-t 2222-2222:22-22'
```
Are you guys sure this is related to selinux/apparmor?

> Are you guys sure this is related to selinux/apparmor?

Thanks a lot for checking. Given your findings, I'm fairly sure it's *not* related to SELinux / AppArmor and it's an actual race condition that happens also with the inotify mechanism pasta uses to exit (that is, not the fallback timer).

This is also consistent with what @Luap99 is seeing on his setup. So, yes, we need to find a way to get rid of that race condition. Some are discussed on #27901, but I have a couple of other ideas, and now that I know this can be reproduced without any Linux Security Module (so, inotify, not timer) I don't have to spend more time trying to reproduce it (it doesn't happen on my setup), I can just make pasta artificially slow. Thanks again.

This should now have a robust workaround in passt [2026_01_20.386b5f5](https://archives.passt.top/passt-user/20260121055331.64707ef8@elisabeth/), see https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27901#issuecomment-3774246685 for details.

The underlying theoretical problem is not fixed, yet.
@sbrivio-rh Happy to have helped, if even only in diagnosing :-) !! Thank you!!
